architecture_summary: Visual Prompt Tuning (VPT) adapts pre-trained vision Transformers by prepending a small number of learnable prompt embeddings in the input space while freezing the entire backbone. It has two variants, VPT-SHALLOW and VPT-DEEP, which insert prompts only at the first layer or at every Transformer layer, respectively.

benchmark_results.table_caption: ViT-B/16 pre-trained on supervised ImageNet-21k. For each method and each downstream task group, we report the average test accuracy score and number of wins in (·) compared to FULL. “Total params” denotes total parameters needed for all 24 downstream tasks. “Scope” denotes the tuning scope of each method. “Extra params” denotes the presence of additional parameters besides the pre-trained backbone and linear head. Best results among all methods except FULL are bolded. VPT outshines the full fine-tuning 20 out of 24 cases with significantly less trainable parameters

Effect of sharing prompts. The top part shows four diagrams of Transformer layers with prompts. The first diagram shows 'Tuned' (orange) and 'Frozen' (grey) prompts. The second diagram shows 'Default' prompts. The third diagram shows 'Shared (intra-layer)' prompts. The fourth diagram shows 'Shared (inter-layer)' prompts. The fifth diagram shows 'Shared (all)' prompts. The bottom part shows three bar charts for VTAB-Natural (7), VTAB-Specialized (4), and VTAB-Structured (8) tasks. Each chart compares 'Full' (orange), 'Default' (blue), 'Default_{i=1}' (blue), 'Shared-intra' (blue), 'Shared-inter' (blue), 'Shared-all' (blue), and 'Linear' (grey) prompt strategies. The charts show that the 'Default' strategy consistently achieves high performance across all tasks, while the 'Linear' strategy consistently achieves low performance.

novel_contributions: Visual Prompt Tuning (VPT) as an efficient and effective alternative to full fine-tuning for large-scale Transformer models in vision
Two variants of VPT: VPT-SHALLOW and VPT-DEEP
VPT introduces only a small amount (less than 1% of model parameters) of trainable parameters in the input space while keeping the model backbone frozen
VPT outperforms full fine-tuning in many cases across model capacities and training data scales
VPT is applied beyond classification to semantic segmentation and to different backbones including ViT, Swin, and ConvNets

key_claims.claim: VPT introduces only a small amount (less than 1% of model parameters) of trainable parameters in the input space while keeping the model backbone frozen.
VPT achieves significant performance gains compared to other parameter efficient tuning protocols and even outperforms full fine-tuning in many cases across model capacities and training data scales.
On 24 downstream recognition tasks spanning different domains using a pre-trained ViT backbone, VPT beats all other transfer learning baselines, even surpassing full fine-tuning in 20 cases.
VPT-DEEP outperforms Full on 3 out of the 4 problem classes (20 out of 24 tasks) while using significantly fewer total model parameters.
VPT-DEEP outperforms all the other parameter-efficient tuning protocols across all task groups.
VPT continues to outperform other parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods on Swin-B and remains competitive on ADE20K semantic segmentation.
Random initialized prompts works better in general than the CLS-based initialization strategy.
